title: Test Case Generator
description: Use when converting a feature spec, bug risk, acceptance criteria, or behavior change into executable test scenarios with preconditions, expected results, priorities, and regression coverage.

test_scopes.Pure function: Deterministic inputs/outputs; cover happy, boundary, invalid input, shape, and regression.
test_scopes.Component/UI: Interaction and rendering; cover keyboard/mouse/touch, loading/empty/error, responsive, and accessibility labels.
test_scopes.API/contract: Request/response behavior; cover auth, validation, idempotency, error schema, compatibility, and limits.
test_scopes.Workflow/E2E: Multi-step user journey; cover critical path, recovery path, persistence, permissions, and integration failure.
test_scopes.Data migration/job: Batch or state transformation; cover idempotency, partial failure, rollback, large data, and retry/resume.
test_scopes.Security/permission: Access control or sensitive data; cover allowed/denied roles, tenant boundary, audit/logging, and leakage.

instructions: Classify test scope and choose relevant case families before generating cases.
Extract testable rules from the spec: actors, inputs, outputs, state transitions, permissions, limits, timing, error handling, integrations, and persistence.
Identify ambiguity before inventing behavior and mark unclear cases as Open Question.
Generate cases across only the relevant families; include a reason when an obvious family is intentionally skipped.
For each case, state preconditions, steps or stimulus, expected result, priority, automation level, and why the case matters.
Prioritize by user impact and failure likelihood: P0 release blocker, P1 core behavior, P2 important edge, P3 low-risk coverage.
Include data setup and teardown needs when state matters.
Keep the set lean, preferring fewer high-signal tests over exhaustive permutations unless the user asks for a full matrix.
Do not write vague expected results like works correctly or test implementation details unless exposed as behavior.

examples.input: Password reset token expires in 15 minutes.
examples.output:
ID | Priority | Scenario | Preconditions | Steps/Input | Expected Result | Type | Automate?
PR-1 | P1 | Token works before expiry | Token issued at T0 | Redeem at T0+14m59s | Password reset succeeds once | Boundary | Yes
PR-2 | P1 | Token expires after limit | Token issued at T0 | Redeem at T0+15m01s | Reset is rejected with expired-token error | Boundary | Yes
PR-3 | P1 | Token cannot be reused | Token already redeemed | Redeem same token again | Reset is rejected and password is unchanged | Regression | Yes
PR-4 | P2 | Expiry is timezone-neutral | Server/user time zones differ | Redeem around DST/timezone boundary | Expiry uses server canonical time, not local display time | Timing | Yes
